LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S (IMPROPERLY SUED AS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT COMMISSION) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 8, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST IMMEDIATELY DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF HER OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-06-2013 CONTINUED TO MAY-08-2013 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
ORDER DENYING MOTION TO DISMISS

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S (IMPROPERLY SUED AS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT COMMISSION) MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS DISCRETION TO ACCEPT A LATE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT OR TO REQUIRE A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE IT. SEE HARLAN V. DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION (2005) 132 CAL.APP.4TH 868. IF THE TARDINESS IS INCONSEQUENTIAL, THE COURT SHOULD DENY THE MOTION TO STRIKE OR DISMISS. HERE, THE COURT DECLINES TO EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO DISMISS THE ACTION BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO TIMELY AMEND HIS COMPLAINT. A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS FILED ON MAY 8, 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUN-26-2013 CONTINUED TO AUG-14-2013 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LIPPIS, RON JURY DEMANDED
EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER ON A STIPULATED TRIAL CONTINUANCE AND STIPULATION FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
OBJECTION AND REQ TO STRIKE PLTF'S JURY FEE DEPOSIT FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION TWO (RETALIATION) AND THREE (HARASSMENT) IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING THAT THERE IS A NEXUS BETWEEN PLAINTIFF BEING MOVED TO THE DAY SHIFT AND PLAINTIFF'S AGE, RACE, OR GENDER. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT COME FORWARD WITH SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE THAT THE CITY'S DECISION TO MOVE HIM TO DAY SHIFT WAS A PRETEXT AND THAT IT'S REAL REASON WAS TO DISCRIMINATE AGAINST HIM. DEFENDANT'S UNOPPOSED REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. 2 ORDERS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WAS SIGNED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 56666157)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S (IMPROPERLY SUED) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. ON THE DISCRIMINATION CAUSE OF ACTION, PAR. 31 ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF WAS SATISFACTORILY PERFORMING HIS JOB. PARS. 8-34 DESCRIBE CONDUCT THAT COULD RISE TO THE LEVEL OF ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. SEE YANOWITZ V. L'OREAL USA, INC. (2005) 36 CAL.4TH 1028, 1059. P SHOULD AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS THAT MORE CLEARLY SHOW A NEXUS BETWEEN THE DISCRIMINATORY ANIMUS AND ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. ON THE RETALIATION CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF SHOULD PLEAD MORE FACTS SHOWING A NEXUS BETWEEN THE PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S OTHER ARGUMENTS ON THIS CLAIM. ON THE HARASSMENT CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF SHOULD MORE CLEARLY ALLEGE THAT THE HARASSING CONDUCT WAS BASED ON AN IMPROPER ANIMUS. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS LEAVE TO ADD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT AND INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WITH THE NAME AND ADDRESS OF THE PREVAILING PARTY'S COUNSEL OR THE PREVAILING PARTY IF PRO PER IN THE TOP LEFT OF THE FIRST PAGE OF PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST BRING THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE HEARING OR EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG PRIOR TO THE HEARING EVEN IF THE MOTION IS NOT OPPOSED OR THE TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT CONTESTED.JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Compendium; Table Of Contents
Off Calendar Aug-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHARLES J. KATZ, TEL. NO. (650) 692-4100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LISA BARNETT SWEEN & ATTY. VALERIE GERARD OF THE LEWIS BRISBOIS & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 262-8566 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication - Motion for summary judgment is granted. As to causes of action two (Retaliation) and three (Harassment) is granted as unopposed. Plaintiff presents no evidence establishing that there is a nexus between Plaintiff being moved to the day shift and Plaintiff's age, race, or gender. Plaintiff has not come forward with substantial evidence that the City's decision to move him to day shift was a pretext and that it's real reason was to discriminate against him. Defendant's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Evidentiary Objections were ruled on the record. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G) and CRC 3.1312. 2 ORDERS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WAS SIGNED BY THE COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Action
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charles J. Katz, Esq. of the Law Offices of Charles J. Katz, Tel. 650.692.4100, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Lisa Barnett Sween, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith, Tel. 415.262.8566, for defendant and moving party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S (IMPROPERLY SUED AS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT COMMISSION) Motion To Dismiss Action is denied. The Court has discretion to accept a late filing of an amended complaint or to require a motion for leave to file it. See Harlan v. Department of Transportation (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 868. If the tardiness is inconsequential, the Court should deny the motion to strike or dismiss. Here, the Court declines to exercise its discretion to dismiss the action based on Plaintiff's failure to timely amend his complaint. A Second Amended Complaint was filed on May 8, 2013. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DAVID LUCERO, ESQ., 415.438.6641, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S (IMPROPERLY SUED) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. On the discrimination cause of action, par. 31 alleges that plaintiff was satisfactorily performing his job. Pars. 8-34 describe conduct that could rise to the level of adverse employment action. See Yanowitz v. L'Oreal USA, Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1028, 1059. P should amend to plead facts that more clearly show a nexus between the discriminatory animus and adverse employment action. On the retaliation cause of action, plaintiff should plead more facts showing a nexus between the protected activity and adverse employment action. The Court rejects defendant's other arguments on this claim. On the harassment cause of action, plaintiff should more clearly allege that the harassing conduct was based on an improper animus. Plaintiff also has leave to add causes of action for negligent and intentional infliction of emotional distress. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
